name: Stuffed Grape Leaves
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Dried parsley or to taste
1/4 teaspoon Black pepper or to taste
1/8 teaspoon Cayenne pepper or to taste
2 cups Cooked white or brown rice
recipeInstructions:
1. In a skillet, heat olive oil and sauté onion and mushrooms until soft.
2. Add parsley and spices. Stir the mixture.
3. Transfer to a bowl and mix in peas and rice.
4. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
5. Line a 3-quart baking dish with a few grape leaves to keep stuffed leaves from sticking and burning.
6. Place about one heaping tablespoon of rice mixture (more or less depending on the size of the leaf) in the center of a grape leaf.
7. Fold in sides, then roll the leaf from stem to tip.
8. Place in casserole dish. Repeat procedure with remaining grape leaves until the rice mixture is used up.
9. Pour water into the bottom of the dish (to prevent sticking and drying out).
10. Bake for 25 minutes.
Serves 12 as an appetizer, or 6 as an entree. Per appetizer serving: 91 calories; 3g protein; 2.5g fat; 14g carbohydrates; 0 cholesterol; 94mg sodium.
